name: L-CiteEval: A Suite for Evaluating Fidelity of Long-context Models (a.k.a. L-CiteEval: Do Long-Context Models Truly Leverage Context for Responding?)
authors: Zecheng Tang, Keyan Zhou, Juntao Li, Baibei Ji, Jianye Hou, Min Zhang — Soochow University / LCM-Lab
dataset_size: 11 tasks across 5 task categories. Total per-task sample count is on the order of 100–200 examples per task in the main set, plus L-CiteEval-Length and L-CiteEval-Hardness variants for controlled ablation. Total main-set size is in the low thousands [uncertain on exact total — paper reports per-task sizes rather than a single headline number].
annotation_unit: Statement-with-citation pair: each model response is required to be emitted as a sequence of statements (S) each followed by a citation (C) pointing to a chunk of the long context. Evaluation is at the statement-citation level, scored via Citation Recall, Citation Precision, and Citation F1. Generation quality is scored separately at the response/answer level using task-specific metrics inherited from the source datasets.
ground_truth_source: Hybrid: gold answers come from the underlying source datasets (NarrativeQA, HotpotQA, 2WikiMultihopQA, NaturalQuestions, GovReport, MultiNews, QMSum, LoCoMo, DialSim, CountingStars, NIAH). Citation correctness is evaluated automatically against the chunked context using a fully automated evaluation suite (no per-example human re-annotation of citations).
judge_model: Automated rule/metric-based evaluation suite for citation precision/recall/F1 (no LLM-judge for citation scoring). For generation quality, task-specific metrics are used (F1, ROUGE, EM as appropriate). The benchmark explicitly aims to be a fully automated evaluation suite without LLM-judge dependence.
domain: Five task categories spanning long-context understanding: (1) single-document QA — NarrativeQA, NaturalQuestions; (2) multi-document QA — HotpotQA, 2WikiMultihopQA; (3) summarization — GovReport, MultiNews, QMSum; (4) dialogue understanding — LoCoMo, DialSim; (5) synthetic / needle-in-haystack — CountingStars, NIAH.
context_length_range: 8K to 48K tokens. The L-CiteEval-Length variant explicitly varies context length to study length-dependent fidelity.
error_taxonomy: No categorical error taxonomy — the framework treats faithfulness as a continuous citation-quality metric (CR / CP / F1). Indirectly, lack of citation = unattributed claim; wrong-chunk citation = mis-attributed claim. L-CiteEval-Hardness adds a difficulty stratification (easy / medium / hard) but this is sample-level difficulty, not an error type.
dynamic_test_generation: false — static curated benchmark built by sampling and adapting from existing long-context source datasets. No dynamic generation mechanism.
contamination_risk: medium-high — built on top of widely-used public source datasets (NarrativeQA, HotpotQA, 2WikiMHQA, NaturalQuestions, GovReport, MultiNews, QMSum, NIAH) that almost certainly appear in pre-training corpora of frontier LLMs. The citation requirement and chunk-level alignment partially mitigate raw-answer leakage, but answer leakage from base datasets remains a real risk for any LLM trained through 2024.
license: Likely permissive (Apache-2.0 or MIT) following the LCM-Lab GitHub conventions, but the LICENSE file content is not explicitly surfaced in search snippets [uncertain]. Underlying source datasets retain their original licenses (NaturalQuestions CC-BY-SA, HotpotQA CC-BY-SA, GovReport custom, etc.).
annotation_cost_per_sample: Near-zero — citations and answers are inherited from existing labeled datasets and the evaluation is fully automated (CR/CP/F1 + task metrics). Only model-inference cost matters.
reported_metric: Citation Recall (CR), Citation Precision (CP), Citation F1; plus task-specific generation-quality metrics (F1 / EM / ROUGE).
reported_value: Closed-source frontier models substantially outperform open-source LCMs on citation precision/recall. RAG significantly improves faithfulness with a small generation-quality cost. Exact headline numbers vary by task and model [uncertain on single headline number — paper reports a per-task table rather than a single aggregate].
overlapping_benchmark: HotpotQA (we already use it in Phase 7) is a direct sub-task of L-CiteEval. NarrativeQA, NaturalQuestions, and 2WikiMHQA are adjacent multi-doc QA settings. This makes L-CiteEval the most direct extension of our Phase 7 multi-hop QA work into the long-context citation regime.
model_used_in_paper: 11 long-context LLMs (≥64K reported context window), mix of closed and open source — including Llama-3.1 family (e.g., Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct), GLM, Qwen-2.5, Mistral long-context variants, and frontier closed models (Claude/GPT). Exact full list is in the paper [uncertain].
applicability: high — perfect fit for Phase 10. Citation-level faithfulness labels at 8K–48K context provide dense supervisory signal at exactly the granularity our spectral H(n) features can address (per-statement entropy windows). HotpotQA overlap means we can directly extend our existing Phase 7 pipeline.
preemption_risk: low — L-CiteEval is purely an evaluation benchmark; it does NOT propose any detection method, does not use entropy / hidden-state / spectral features, and treats the LCM as a black box. Our spectral fusion is fully orthogonal. The benchmark provides labels (citation correctness) that we can use as ground truth for our detector.
bracha_alignment: high — Citation precision/recall/F1 are textbook targets for conformal-style risk control; LTT can give distribution-free bounds on miscitation rate. PPI is natural because automated CR/CP/F1 is itself a noisy metric (chunk-level alignment can mis-credit), and Bracha's machinery handles exactly this noisy-label setting.
ofir_alignment: moderate-to-high — long context (8K–48K) generates long entropy / hidden-state trajectories suitable for multi-view kernel / spectral methods. The statement-with-citation structure naturally segments the trajectory into per-statement windows, which is the right unit for sw_var_peak / STFT analysis.
notes: Strong candidate as the FIRST benchmark to extend our spectral pipeline into long-doc territory because: (1) HotpotQA overlap provides continuity with Phase 7; (2) automated CR/CP/F1 evaluation means no judge-API cost; (3) Llama-3.1-8B is in our white-box-friendly model class; (4) data is on HuggingFace and code on GitHub. Caveat: contamination risk is non-trivial for HotpotQA / NaturalQuestions on any 2024+ model. Recommend pairing with a more recently-curated benchmark (FACTS Grounding v2 or DeepHalluBench) to triangulate.
model_size_range: Open-source models tested span ~7B–70B (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct explicitly mentioned); closed-source frontier judges/evaluees as well. Our Falcon-3-10B and Qwen-7B/72B are in-distribution.
gpu_requirements: Long-context inference at 32K–48K tokens. On Colab A100 80GB: 7B/8B models fit comfortably at 48K; 70B requires AWQ/GPTQ quantization (already in our setup); KV cache at 48K is the dominant memory bottleneck.
sample_size_for_replication: Per-task subsets of ~100 examples are sufficient for stable CR/CP/F1 estimates; the paper's reported per-task sizes are in this range.
min_samples_for_replication: ~50 examples per task gives a noisy but interpretable result; full main set across the 11 tasks is on the order of 1K–2K examples [uncertain].
runtime_estimate: Long-context generation at 8K–48K context: tens of seconds to a few minutes per example on A100 for 7B–13B models; 70B AWQ slower. Citation scoring is millisecond-cheap (string-match + chunk index).
